mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding consumer credit balances signal synchronized demand recovery across durable goods and services, which lifts equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the industrial production index indicate expanding manufacturing capacity and improving profit margins, which directly supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 2.0% annualized for two consecutive quarters, indicating supply overshoot and margin compression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, driving positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days as institutional investors adjust portfolios to reflect stronger macroeconomic momentum.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, or the rolling correlation with forward returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points within three months signals aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in over 65% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance after adjusting for CPIAUCSL inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging employment trends where payroll employment growth slows while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously signal labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer spending power and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in out-of-sample periods, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for RSAFS credit conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts above 600 thousand units reflects strong builder confidence and household formation, which stimulates downstream economic activity and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when HOUST exceeds 700 thousand units for three consecutive months, indicating overheating and subsequent Federal Reserve policy reversal.
